summary: Implemented a post-install Codex hook approval flow. The installer now creates DinoBrain Codex Hook Approval.cmd and launches it after hook registration unless skipped. The helper detects stale Codex desktop processes, restarts/reopens Codex, copies /hooks to the clipboard, and shows instructions while preserving the requirement that the user manually trusts the hook. Updated the Windows setup UI with a checked-by-default approval flow option, added verification, updated uninstall cleanup, bumped version to 0.1.4, pushed main/tag, uploaded the v0.1.4 release ZIP, and verified the uploaded private asset hash.
decisions: Do not bypass Codex hook trust. The helper only opens the /hooks flow and requires the user to click trust/approve.
Default installer behavior launches the hook approval helper after hook registration; users can opt out with -SkipCodexRestartFlow or the GUI checkbox.
The approval helper restarts only stale Codex desktop processes whose StartTime predates hooks.json, not arbitrary codex command runner processes.
New hook approval launcher is included in purge uninstall cleanup.
next_steps: Install from v0.1.4 ZIP on the target PC; after the helper opens Codex, run or paste /hooks and approve DinoBrain.
